reviews.review_description:
ShapeLab is my main tool for work now, and I honestly can’t imagine my workflow without it anymore. It just feels natural in a way very few tools do. What makes it even better is the team behind it - the developers are super active and are constantly improving the software, which really shows.

And honestly, I still think VR sculpting is the best thing that has ever happened to VR :)) It doesn’t feel like a gimmick - it feels like something truly powerful, inspiring, and genuinely useful for artists.

reviews.review_title: Best VR Sculpting software out there
reviews.review_description:
As a traditional sculptor, I always felt something was missing when I first transitioned into digital work. That changed with Shapelab. It bridges the gap beautifully, offering an experience that genuinely feels like sculpting in real life, just inside a headset. The interface is intuitive and easy to pick up, without sacrificing depth or control.

What really stands out is the sheer amount of processing power it handles while running entirely on a standalone headset, with no need for a PC. It’s remarkably freeing. I can take my studio anywhere, on a train, in a car, even on a plane and continue working without compromise. For anyone serious about digital sculpting, especially those coming from a traditional background, it’s an impressive and thoughtfully designed tool.

reviews.review_title: A Must-Have for every Digital Sculptor!
reviews.review_description:
Shapelab Max standalone brings everything I love about the PCVR version into a portable format. The freedom to sculpt anywhere without being tied to a PC is quite amazing. There’s no cable setup or desk commitment. You just put the headset on and start creating.

Performance will always depend on the headset hardware, but considering it’s running standalone, I’ve been extremely impressed. The passthrough mode especially has changed how I use it. Being able to sculpt while still seeing my surroundings feels natural and practical. I can stay present, talk with my family, and still get work done without feeling cut off from the world.

For me, it’s worth every penny, and I can’t recommend it enough. Sculpting in VR is by far the most intuitive way to create. It feels much closer to traditional sculpting, where you can truly see and shape the forms in front of you. Shapelab Max makes that experience accessible wherever you are.

Shapelab has been a game changer for me. I cannot recommend it enough. I've used it to create custom miniatures for my board game company and they are selling well, and I've since created a successful ETSY store where I create custom dnd miniatures using Shapelab. I never quite got my head around other software untill I found Shapelab. There's a wonderfully helpful and vibrant dischord channel and fantastic tech support. 10/10

This app is absolutely brilliant, it brings the full power of shapelabs running directly on the quest3, and the quality is really good, the only limitation compared to the pc version is the power of the standalone headset, but even than the app is so well balanced that it runs great. Not to mention that you can work entirely in AR, definitely a must have for any 3d artist that owns a quest3

Shapelab Max is awesome !
It’s really easy, comfortable and fun to use.

It’s also powerfull, I thought it would only handle a low polygons count but I went far over 1 million and it stayed smooth enought to be comfortable to use !

I also tried to make it crash on purpose by making booleans between two weirdly shaped complex objects that were over 800,000 polygons each but it didn’t crash and the operation was quickly resolved!

I used the desktop computer version of Shapelab a lot and this standalone headset version offers the same tools.

Shapelab is a dream come true for sculptors!

reviews.review_title: The freedom of creation flows freely
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely incredible product. I had the desktop version and intended to use it wired with a VR headset, but once I saw that they would be supporting the full version wirelessly on Quest, I had to try that first. 

I was blown away by how quickly it dropped me into creating, and how intuitive the controls were to pick up. 

11/10, would happily purchase again (and I have, along with another headset for my wife)

Whether you're transitioning from physical clay or standard 3D software, working in VR is a fantastic shift, and Shapelab makes it feel natural from the start. The menu design is intuitive enough that you're in the flow within minutes, and once your hands adapt, grabbing and manipulating your sculpt directly just clicks in a way that's hard to describe until you've tried it. It cuts out the usual software hurdles and gets you straight to the fun part.

Shapelab on PC VR is an amazing sculpting tool. I wasnt expecting much from this standalone version but it has far exceeded expectations! It's not a game but extremely powerful and intuitive after watching a couple tutorials 🙂

Alright. I'm pretty new to this  3D modeling thing, so maybe I'm not the most qualified to write a review for this product. But I have had Shapelab on my PC since the latter half of 2025, and I do have the Shapelab Max standalone for the MetaQuest 3S, and I can say so far the experience of using both has been largely comparable to each other.

All the tools and smooth operation of the PCVR version is here in the VR standalone, with not much slowdown to note. I was able to make a little quickly constructed ruined temple on a cliff face, with a big experimentation blob testing out all the tools in one scene and did not notice a frame rate drop. I think my wi-fi quality had more to do with the performance of my headset then the software, really.

I think Shapelab is a great choice for those getting into 3D sculpting or modeling, as Shapelab is much more affordable then Z-brush and much less complex then Blender. It reaches a happy medium that any creative mind can appreciate, with it's simple to grasp tools and jump-in-and-play interactivity. Also, I think it's worth noting that any alphas and stamp templates from the PCVR version can be ported over to the VR standalone via a link cable and a file explorer. No need to think that one version is missing out much from the other. The only exception perhaps being matcaps, but those aren't usually a big thing that's a concern for a finished model anyhow.

If you're a creative person who's itching to get into 3D sculpting, you could do much worse then try out Shapelab. It might just be one of the best choices out there.
This tool is a powerful example of what’s possible inside a VR/XR.  I found the mixed reality sculpting and the custom stamps tools really awesome for kit bashing workflows.
Coming from blender, using Shapelab Max took no time at all to readjust to the controls with the addition of a shortcut wheels makes every option/brushes easily accessible.
